Question: In your own words, explain how to determine whether a sequence is arithmetic.
o3 Answer:
To tell if a sequence is arithmetic, look at how the terms change from one to the next.

1. Pick any term (except the first) and subtract the term that comes right before it.  
   Example: for 7, 11, 15, 19 … do 11 − 7 = 4, 15 − 11 = 4, 19 − 15 = 4.

2. Do the same subtraction for every consecutive pair in the list.

3. If the result—called the common difference—is exactly the same every time, the sequence is arithmetic.  
   • Same number each time ➔ arithmetic.  
   • Different numbers at any point ➔ not arithmetic.

In short, an arithmetic sequence is one in which you can keep adding (or subtracting) the same fixed amount to get from each term to the next.

   term₂ – term₁,  
   term₃ – term₂,  
   term₄ – term₃,  and so on.
